_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of key management service (KMS) principles and practices
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of IT service management principles as applied to data services, including service catalog design, service level management, incident and problem management, and continual service improvement.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI OP domain. Knowledge OP-K-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-K-019 (DQ, cosine: 0.453), DQ-K-016 (DQ, cosine: 0.432).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.3/10 (raw 0.628). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, management, principles, service]. NICE-only terms [key, kms, practices] vs WIDAI-only terms [applied, catalog, continual, data, design, improvement] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.872 (P=0.878, R=0.867). Balanced P/R — the 9-word NICE element and 26-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Knowledge of key management service (KMS) principles and practices" and "Knowledge of IT service management principles as applied to data services, inclu" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.580 ≈ STS 0.628 (gap 0.048). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [knowledge, management, principles, service] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 26.1%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, management, principles, service]. NICE-only: [key, kms, practices]. WIDAI-only: [applied, catalog, continual, data, design, improvement].
